source.title: Quantifying cerebral contributions to pain beyond nociception

confidence_rationale: Strong source (Nat Comm, N=137 training + 46 independent test, cross-validated, mediation of psychological manipulations). The SIIPS1 is explicitly orthogonalised against nociceptive input -- it captures pain-relevant variance that the NPS misses, loading on NAcc, lateral PFC, parahippocampal cortex. This is direct evidence that affective/contextual pain and sensory-discriminative pain have separable cerebral contributions, which is the empirical structure that ARC-058 claims -- a shared upstream surprise computation plus stream-specific signed readouts. Supports the partitioned-head side of ARC-058 (which is where the z_harm_s vs z_harm_a split actually lives in the REE spec).
failure_signatures: If pain were fully captured by a single shared forward-model trunk with no per-stream heads, the SIIPS1 should be near-zero after controlling for nociceptive input. It is not: SIIPS1 explains trial-by-trial pain variance that NPS misses and mediates psychological manipulations of expectation and perceived control.

If pain were fully partitioned into two independent streams with no shared substrate, we would not expect the specific partial overlap that NPS + SIIPS1 show in anterior insula. The partial overlap is compatible with ARC-058's trunk-plus-heads structure, not with pure ARC-033 independence.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A multivariate pattern signature (SIIPS1) trained to predict trial-by-trial pain variance NOT captured by a nociceptive signature (NPS) generalises across samples and mediates the pain-modulating effects of expectation and perceived-control manipulations. SIIPS1 loads on NAcc, lateral PFC, parahippocampal cortex, and other regions largely non-overlapping with NPS in posterior insula and S2.
mapping.ree_translation: SIIPS1 is the closest empirical analogue to z_harm_a in REE: the affective/contextual component of pain that is separable from the sensory-discriminative nociceptive readout. The fact that a cross-validated pattern exists AT ALL for this component argues for a dedicated per-stream head in ARC-058 (or an independent forward model in ARC-033). The partial AIC overlap between NPS and SIIPS1 is the biological signature of ARC-058's shared trunk + stream-specific heads architecture. Under pure ARC-033, SIIPS1 should overlap MINIMALLY with NPS even in AIC; under ARC-058, a partial AIC overlap is expected and observed. The direction for ARC-058 is therefore supports.
mapping.mapping_caveat: SIIPS1 is an affective/contextual pain signature but not EXCLUSIVELY a forward-model PE readout -- it captures expectation and perceived-control contributions, which is broader than z_harm_a's spec. The paper does not compute PE directly; it computes the residual after orthogonalising against NPS. Inferences about shared-trunk architecture are made by spatial comparison of signature maps, not by direct forward-model fitting.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI, four training studies (N=137), two independent test samples (N=46), thermal and pressure pain, machine-learning multivariate pattern analysis with explicit orthogonalisation against nociceptive input.

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality. Mapping fidelity is good because SIIPS1 is the closest existing human-fMRI analogue of the partitioned affective-pain head ARC-058 posits. Transfer risk is moderate -- SIIPS1 captures expectation and perceived-control variance, which is broader than z_harm_a proper; the paper's decoder is trained against realised pain ratings, not against an explicit forward-model PE.
